name: create_event
description: Create an event on the operator's Google Calendar. Provide a summary (title) and start/end times. Times accept an RFC3339 datetime (e.g. '2026-06-20T09:00:00-07:00' or '...Z') for a timed event, or a plain date (e.g. '2026-06-20') for an all-day event. Returns the created event's id, htmlLink, status, summary, start, and end.

start.description: Start time. RFC3339 datetime (contains 'T', e.g. '2026-06-20T09:00:00Z') for a timed event, OR an all-day date (e.g. '2026-06-20').

end.description: End time. Same format as start: RFC3339 datetime for a timed event, OR an all-day date (for all-day events this is the EXCLUSIVE end date).

calendar_id.description: Optional. Target calendar id (default 'primary'). Use list_calendars to discover other calendar ids.

attendees.description: Optional. List of attendee email addresses to invite.

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

returns: JSON {id, htmlLink, status, summary, start, end} for the created event (or an error string).
notes: Requires Google Workspace connected for the operator (Calendar scope granted by the Google sign-in in onboarding). For a timed event include 'T' and a timezone offset (or 'Z') in start/end; for an all-day event pass dates with no 'T' (the end date is exclusive). Use list_calendars to target a calendar other than 'primary'.
